title: angelica sylvestris var. litoralis
intro: Angelica sylvestris var. litoralis, also known as sea angelica, is a coastal variety of the angelica plant, characterized by its tall, stately appearance and fragrant foliage. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, sea angelica has been valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating a range of ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The plant's medicinal properties also contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, sea angelica is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
